free_text: Bimodal fission in binary and ternary spontaneous
fission of 252Cf
Ba-Mo and Ba-alpha-Zr fission neutron yields
Gammasphere detector array located at Lawrence
Berkeley National Laboratory
A 35 mu-Ci 252Cf source was deposited as a 5-mm spot on
a 1.8-mu-m Ti foil covered on both sides by gold foils.
These foils were of thickness 3.7 mu-m on the front and
4.6 mu-m on the back.
Triple gamma coincidence and LCP-gamma-
gamma coincidence techniques.
The binary data were taken in November 2000. A 62
mu-Ci source was placed between two iron foils to
stop fission fragments. This arrangement was then put
in a 7.62-cm polyethylene ball and placed in
Gammasphere. The ternary data were taken in December
72 Compton-suppressed Ge detectors.
Gammasphere calibrated with 133Ba, 137Cs, 152Eu, 56Co,
and 57Co sources.
Eight deltaE-E detectors were placed
around the source to detect light-charged particles.
Four were placed at azimuthal angles of 45, 135, 225,
and 135 degrees with a polar angle of 30 degrees,
while the other four were placed at the same
azimuthal angles with a polar angle of 150 degrees.
The delta-E detectors had an area of 10 x 10 mm with
a thickness of 10 mu-m, while the E detectors had an
area of 20 x 20 mm with a thickness of 400 mu-m.
Data corrected for detector efficiency and internal
conversion coefficients.
OG.
Relative
yield matrix for the Ba-Mo charge split.
No information on source of uncertainties
Data from Tab.I of Phys.Rev.C74,017309,2006.
Preliminary data in Phys.of Atom.Nucl.69,1161,2006.
Relative
yield matrix for the Ba-alpha-Zr split.
No information on source of uncertainties
Data from Tab.II of Phys.Rev.C74,017309,2006.
Preliminary data in Phys.of Atom.Nucl.69,1161,2006.
Only the strongest transitions to ground states
were measured. The yields for other transitions
to ground states were calculated using known values
of relative intensities.
The total yield for each Xe gate nucleus was normalized
to total yield in binary fission using tables of Wahl.
No information on source of uncertainties
Data from Tab.1 in the 2-nd reference
Single gates were set on the strongest transitions in
132-136Te nuclei, and the intensities of the
strongest transition in 106-112Ru nuclei were
measured. Since there is no absolute yield known for
ternary fission, the total yield for each nucleus was
normalized to the binary yields.
Te-alpha-Ru ternary fission neutron yields (Fig.3 in
the 2-nd ref.)
No information on source of uncertainties
Data from Tab.3 in the 2-nd reference
The gate nuclei were 136-142Xe, and relative yields
of 102-109Mo nuclei were measured.
Xe-alpha-Mo ternary fission neutron yields (Fig.4 in
the 2-nd ref.)
No information on source of uncertainties
Data from Tab.4 in the 2-nd reference
